_doc: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the Zigbee byte-contract shared by firmware (C), the Z2M converter (JS) and the HA docs. Edit ONLY this file, then run `node contract/codegen.mjs`. CI fails if the generated files drift (contract/contract.test.mjs). This project: ESP32-C6 Super Mini + BME680 + Waveshare Solar Power Manager -> sleepy Zigbee end device for Home Assistant.

device.description: ESP32-C6 solar environment sensor: BME680 (T/RH/P/gas) + battery telemetry, deep-sleep Zigbee end device
device._powerSourceNote: 0x03 = Battery. Solar-charged Li-ion behind the Waveshare Solar Power Manager. Sleepy-end-device role comes from the ZED build + rx_on_when_idle=false, not from this cosmetic field.

_manufacturerCodeNote: 0x131B = Espressif Systems' allocated ZCL manufacturer code. MUST be identical in firmware (on the wire) and in the converter, or manufacturer-specific attribute reads/writes silently fail as 'attribute not found'.

customCluster.name: biometalEnviro
customCluster._idNote: 0xFC00 — manufacturer-specific cluster range (0xFC00-0xFFFF). DOWN config writes only; UP telemetry rides STANDARD clusters (see _upPathNote).

_upPathNote: Lesson inherited from c6-radiometer (proven live 2026-07-11): the Z2M addon environment cannot decode INCOMING custom-cluster frames (endpoint->registry lookup loses the attached cluster; msg.cluster undefined; no fz converter matches). So ALL device->HA telemetry travels on STANDARD clusters: Temperature 0x0402 / Humidity 0x0405 / Pressure 0x0403 / PowerConfig 0x0001 on EP1, plus one genAnalogInput (0x000C) endpoint per non-standard channel (gas resistance, status flags, precise vbat, wake counter). The custom cluster carries ONLY the HA->device config writes below (outgoing framing from the converter is proven to work).
_exposeNote: `expose` is the canonical HA-facing property name: the Z2M expose `name` (-> HA entity object_id) AND, for `down` attrs, the key in `zigbee2mqtt/<device>/set`.

attributes.name: statusFlags
attributes.desc: Sensor + power status bitmask (see statusBits); mirrored on AI EP4

attributes.name: wakeCount
attributes.desc: Deep-sleep wake counter since power-on; increments every cycle, so HA sees one report per wake

attributes.name: vbatMv
attributes.desc: Battery voltage, millivolts (precise; PowerConfig 0x0020 only has 100 mV steps)

attributes.name: awakeMs
attributes.desc: Duration of the previous wake cycle, ms (deep-sleep duty-cycle diagnostic)

attributes.name: gasResistance
attributes.desc: BME680 gas sensor resistance, ohms (higher = cleaner air); mirrored on AI EP2

attributes.name: reportIntervalS
attributes.desc: Deep-sleep measurement/report period, seconds (3 s default; raise to 60+ for battery-only operation)

attributes.name: gasEnabled
attributes.desc: Run the BME680 gas heater each cycle (heater burns ~12 mA for 150 ms; disable to save battery)

_analogEndpointsNote: One STANDARD genAnalogInput endpoint per non-standard channel, value in presentValue (SINGLE float, stack-reported). Integers ride the float exactly (all < 2^24). wakeCount changes every cycle -> guarantees at least one report per wake, so HA `last_seen` tracks the 3 s cadence even when the environment is static. The endpoint budget is deliberately capped at EP1..EP5: field evidence on 2026-07-23 showed that adding T/RH/P mirrors on EP6..EP8 regressed a previously successful 23 s interview into repeated active-endpoint/simple-descriptor timeouts on this sleepy ZED. T/RH/P remain on their standard EP1 clusters.

statusBits.name: sensor_error
statusBits.desc: BME680 not detected or measurement failed this cycle

statusBits.name: heater_unstable
statusBits.desc: Gas heater did not reach stability — gas_resistance unreliable this cycle

statusBits.name: vbat_invalid
statusBits.desc: Battery ADC read failed (check the divider wiring)

statusBits.name: gas_disabled
statusBits.desc: Gas heater disabled via HA (gas_enabled = OFF)

statusBits.name: first_boot
statusBits.desc: This cycle is a cold boot / reset, not a deep-sleep wake

_batteryLowMvNote: Below this the battery_low status bit is raised. The %-curve lives in firmware/main/cycle.c (host-tested piecewise Li-ion curve).
_awakeWindowNote: After a factory-new join, a BOOT-button press, or a non-timer cold boot that restores the existing Zigbee network from NVRAM, the device stays awake this long with continuous RX so the Zigbee2MQTT interview can complete. Timer deep-sleep wakes remain on the normal sleepy path.
